channel.title: AI Firehose: What Is New
channel.description: We read the whole AI firehose so you do not have to, then distill it into one brief: what is new, what is breaking out, and what is worth your time, across the day, week, month, and quarter. You take what you want and leave the rest.

item.title: Building with ADK & A2A: How multi-agent collaboration designs a game
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud demonstrates multi-agent collaboration using Agent Development Kit (ADK) and Agent2Agent protocol (A2A) to coordinate AI agents across architecture, asset rendering, and project management tasks. The demonstration builds a game using a human-in-the-loop framework with agents deployed on Cloud Run.

item.title: "ChatGPT Moment" for Robotics Is Coming. The Real Problem Isn't Intelligence | Stanford, Catie Cuan
item.description: EO: Catie Cuan argues that a "ChatGPT moment" is coming for robotics but warns against over-focusing on humanoid robots as the sole vision for the future. She advocates for designing robots prioritizing human interaction and delight over utility alone, suggesting that billions of robots will reshape how we approach robotic design and deployment.

item.title: Is Unstructured Data The Key To Successful AI Deployments?
item.description: Alex Kantrowitz: A podcast discussion featuring Hyland executives and Erie Insurance's CIO explores how unstructured data is critical to successful enterprise AI deployments, particularly in agentic systems. The conversation highlights how proper use of unstructured data helps overcome failures in AI pilots and token allocation, enabling real business impact in healthcare, banking, and insurance.

item.title: You Might Not Need 50 Diffusion Steps — Ziv Ilan, Nvidia
item.description: AI Engineer: NVIDIA's approach reduces diffusion model denoising steps from fifty to as few as one by combining dynamic quantization, latent chunk caching to skip recomputation, and student-teacher distillation. The technique achieves ten to two hundred times speedup enabling near real-time video generation on Blackwell B200, with gains described as additive so methods can be applied independently or stacked.

item.title: Gemini Omni Tutorial: How to Use Gemini Omni AI for Beginners
item.description: AI Master: Tutorial covering Google Omni Flash for AI video editing workflows. The content demonstrates real prompts, generation failures, iterative edits, character consistency tests, and comparisons with Sora. Key focus is whether Omni Flash represents a paradigm shift toward editable video generation versus traditional one-shot generation.

item.title: Kagenti’s Approach to Multi-Agent Security for AI Agents
item.description: IBM Technology: Kagenti is an open source infrastructure for multi-agent security that uses identity and delegation chains to prevent data leaks in agentic AI systems. It implements identity-based security and zero-trust principles to protect multi-agent deployments in production.

item.title: This command SAVED my broken AI coding session
item.description: JavaScript Mastery: A YouTube video from JavaScript Mastery describes a command that resolved issues in an AI-assisted coding workflow. The specific command and its mechanics are detailed in the full video.

item.title: Tencent Workbuddy: RIP Claude,Codex! This is ACTUALLY quite a bit CRAZY!
item.description: AICodeKing: Tencent WorkBuddy is a desktop AI agent designed for productivity work including PDF reading, spreadsheet analysis, report generation, and presentation creation. It offers 100+ domain expert agents, supports multi-agent workflows, integrates with tools like GitHub and Slack, and can be controlled remotely via messaging apps.

item.title: Quoting Matteo Wong, The Atlantic
item.description: Simon Willison: Katie Moussouris reviewed the White House's report on the Fable jailbreak and characterized the model's behavior as working as intended for cyberdefense. Fable initially refused to review insecure code for security issues but complied with a rephrased request to fix the code, which Moussouris interpreted as normal model behavior rather than a security failure.

item.title: Microsoft turns to AWS as GitHub faces AI capacity crunch
item.description: ilreb: Microsoft has turned to AWS infrastructure to address AI capacity constraints at GitHub. This move highlights infrastructure scaling challenges as GitHub's AI features face demand exceeding current capacity.

item.title: Anthropic Sued over Alleged False Advertising on Claude Max Subscription Limits
item.description: SilverElfin: Anthropic faces a lawsuit alleging false advertising regarding its Claude Max subscription limits. The suit challenges claims made about the service's capabilities and tier restrictions.

item.title: [AINews] Satya on Loopcraft: Building Frontier Ecosystems
item.description: Latent Space: Satya Nadella has published an essay on Loopcraft and building frontier ecosystems in AI. The piece reflects on approaches to developing advanced AI capabilities and the surrounding infrastructure needs.

item.title: The White House Is Ratcheting Up Its War Against Anthropic
item.description: Filligree: The White House is intensifying its opposition to Anthropic. The article discusses escalating tensions between U.S. government and the AI company.

item.title: Cloudflare CAPTCHA on at least one ampersand
item.description: Simon Willison: Simon Willison shared a configuration trick for Cloudflare's managed challenge CAPTCHA to only trigger on search URLs with multiple query parameters, avoiding false positives on simple searches. He demonstrated using Claude Code and the Cloudflare MCP for rule configuration, then switched to the Cloudflare API when the MCP proved insufficient.

item.title: Sundar Pichai faces boos, walkout at Stanford graduation ceremony over Google’s Israel, ICE ties
item.description: TechCrunch AI: Sundar Pichai, Google's CEO, faced protests at Stanford's graduation ceremony from students objecting to Google's involvement in defense contracts related to Israel and ICE. The incident reflects ongoing debate about AI technology's application in controversial government and military uses.

item.title: Show HN: Claude Code for Visual Studio (native diff with accept/reject)
item.description: firish: Claude Code for Visual Studio is an IDE extension that implements the Claude Code protocol for native integration with the Claude CLI. It displays code edits in Visual Studio's native diff viewer with accept/reject buttons, automatically shares compiler errors and selected text for context, and includes session monitoring and optional auto-accept features.

item.title: Run Gemma on the edge with the Coral Board
item.description: Google for Developers: Google's Coral Board is a low-power development board with a Coral NPU accelerator designed to run lightweight models like Gemma on edge devices. The board ships with I/O peripherals including screen, camera, microphones, and LEDs, and supports applications like live translation, voice control, and multimodal inference entirely on-device.

item.title: Google Just Revealed What Comes After AGI And It’s Shocking
item.description: AI Revolution: Google DeepMind released a paper arguing that AGI may be a starting point rather than an endpoint, with the transition to ASI potentially mattering more than AGI itself. The paper suggests that once human-level AI can be copied, sped up, and networked into agent teams to build better AI, intelligence could become an industrializable process.

item.title: Prediction and Entropy of Printed English - Claude Shannon (1950) [pdf]
item.description: consumer451: Claude Shannon's 1950 paper introduces methods for quantifying the entropy and predictability of printed English text. The work establishes information-theoretic foundations for understanding language structure through prediction and statistical analysis.

item.title: AWS WAF now lets content owners charge AI bots for access
item.description: mak8: AWS WAF has introduced a new feature that enables content owners to charge AI bots for access to their resources. This creates a monetization mechanism for controlling and managing AI bot traffic on protected websites.

item.title: Show HN: When Will AI? – A timeline of top AI predictions
item.description: jaymeh13: A Hacker News post compiling a timeline of notable AI predictions from researchers and industry figures. The project collects forecasts about when major AI capabilities and milestones will be achieved.

item.title: The US government’s Anthropic models ban was never about an AI jailbreak
item.description: TechCrunch AI: The Trump administration forced Anthropic to pull its cybersecurity models. The author argues the ban was motivated by reactionary or retaliatory political concerns rather than genuine security issues, suggesting government interference in the AI industry.

item.title: Stop testing your SAAS manually
item.description: Jack Roberts: KaneAI is an AI-powered testing automation tool that runs in the background and generates, executes, and validates tests from plain English descriptions. It replaces manual QA processes and reportedly cuts testing costs by 50%.

item.title: Anthropic pauses credit change for Claude Code
item.description: fabianlindfors: Anthropic announced a pause on a previously planned change to separate billing for Claude Agent SDK and claude-p from subscription rate limits. Users will continue operating under current subscription terms with no immediate changes while the company revises its approach.

item.title: This MCP makes Hermes Agent 10x more powerful
item.description: David Ondrej: Apify MCP Connectors are a set of Model Context Protocol integrations that significantly enhance the capabilities of the Hermes Agent by providing expanded tool access and functionality.

item.title: How to build reliable software with AI agents
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: A video discussion examining whether AI-powered coding agents reduce technical debt or increase maintenance burden. The speakers argue that human-in-the-loop verification, rigorous code review, and strong engineering practices are essential to ensure AI agents improve quality rather than just accelerate output.

item.title: LLMs Will Replace 8-Track Duplication Engineers
item.description: wanderingjew: LLMs are predicted to replace 8-track duplication engineers as a job category. The claim draws a historical parallel to an obsolete technology to predict labor displacement in a narrowly specialized field.

item.title: Why do South Koreans love AI so much?
item.description: MIT Technology Review AI: MIT Technology Review examines South Korea's widespread enthusiasm for AI adoption across society. The piece opens with anecdotes of AI applications in everyday infrastructure like immigration checkpoints and public transit.

item.title: The AI Price War Is Here, Piling Pressure on OpenAI and Anthropic
item.description: AnodicElegy: The AI market is experiencing intensifying price competition that creates pressure on leading LLM providers. This commentary suggests competitive dynamics are reshaping the industry landscape for companies like OpenAI and Anthropic.

item.title: Meta’s new ‘AI Mode’ on Facebook pulls from public info across its platforms
item.description: TechCrunch AI: Meta announced a rollout of new AI features called 'AI Mode' on Facebook that leverages public information across its platforms. The announcement represents Meta's effort to compete in the AI race while increasing user engagement on the platform.

item.title: SpaceX is public: Everything you need to know post-IPO
item.description: TechCrunch AI: TechCrunch provides comprehensive coverage of SpaceX's initial public offering, including analysis of winners and losers post-IPO, pre-IPO financial dealings, and details from the company's S-1 registration filing. The coverage traces SpaceX's evolution from early challenges through current success.

item.title: Claude Fable 5 is Banned... Do THIS Right Now
item.description: Jack Roberts: Jack Roberts demonstrates how to run open-source AI models locally using Ollama and build a free NotebookLM alternative with open notebook, in response to Claude Fable 5 being withdrawn. The video covers model routing via OpenRouter and strategies to maintain access to multiple models without cloud dependencies.

item.title: Show HN: A pure-Ruby X11 terminal
item.description: vidarh: A pure-Ruby X11 terminal emulator with complete font rendering, X11 bindings, and VT-100/VT-102 support built entirely in Ruby. It supports double-width/double-height text, Unicode, special box-drawing character rendering, and includes an ANSI text backend for nested terminal operation.

item.title: Fine-Tuning Biological Foundation Models with LoRA Using NVIDIA BioNeMo Recipes
item.description: NVIDIA Developer: NVIDIA Developer blog describes fine-tuning biological foundation models using LoRA with BioNeMo Recipes. The post covers application of efficient adaptation techniques to pretrained models like ESM2 for computational biology tasks.

item.title: The Value Axis: Language Models Encode Whether They're on the Right Track
item.description: Nick Jiang, Isaac Kauvar, Jack Lindsey: Researchers identify a "value axis" in language model activations that encodes the model's assessment of trajectory success. They use this axis to show that steering activations causally modulates self-correction, confidence, and backtracking behavior across synthetic and real-world settings.

item.title: Context-Aware RL for Agentic and Multimodal LLMs
item.description: Peiyang Xu, Bangzheng Li, Sijia Liu: ContextRL improves long-horizon reasoning and multimodal performance in LLMs by training models to select contexts that support query-answer pairs through an auxiliary objective, rather than only supervising final answers. The method is evaluated on long-horizon agent benchmarks and visual question answering tasks.

item.title: BRDFusion: Physics Meets Generation for Urban Scene Inverse Rendering
item.description: Yi-Ruei Liu, Jie-Ying Lee, Zheng-Hui Huang: BRDFusion combines physics-based rendering with generative models to recover scene properties from videos and perform controllable inverse rendering. The framework enables applications including novel-view relighting, night simulation, and dynamic object editing while maintaining physical consistency and controllability.

item.title: Exact Posterior Score Estimation for Solving Linear Inverse Problems
item.description: Abbas Mammadov, Ozgur Kara, Kaan Oktay: The authors derive the exact posterior score in closed form for linear Gaussian inverse problems using diffusion and flow-based models. They introduce EPS, a denoising training objective that preserves pretrained denoiser structure and reduces posterior sampling to a shifted denoising problem with anisotropic noise covariance. EPS outperforms training-free and training-based baselines on linear inverse problems while using substantially fewer denoiser evaluations.

item.title: Geometric Action Model for Robot Policy Learning
item.description: Jisang Han, Seonghu Jeon, Jaewoo Jung: Geometric Action Model (GAM) is a language-conditioned manipulation policy that repurposes a pretrained geometric foundation model by splitting it at an intermediate layer. Shallow layers encode observations while a causal future predictor forecasts future latent tokens conditioned on language and proprioception, enabling both 3D geometric reasoning and action decoding with minimal architectural modification.

item.title: Hierarchical Advantage Weighting for Online RL Fine-Tuning of VLAs from Sparse Episode Outcomes
item.description: Tongyan Fang, Siyuan Huang, Naiyu Fang: Hierarchical Advantage-Weighted Behavior Cloning (HABC) fine-tunes pretrained VLA policies using online RL by training separate critic heads for viability and efficiency objectives on different data subsets. A state-adaptive gate merges their advantages and converts outputs into per-transition weights, with intervention-aware credit assignment preventing supervision leakage across policy intervention boundaries.

item.title: Benchmarking LLM Agents on Meta-Analysis Articles from Nature Portfolio
item.description: Anzhe Xie, Weihang Su, Yujia Zhou: MetaSyn is a dataset of 442 expert-curated meta-analyses from Nature Portfolio journals designed to benchmark LLM agents on evidence synthesis tasks. It pairs research questions with PI/ECO criteria, retrieval corpora of 140k PubMed articles, verified positive studies, and hard negatives to evaluate the full retrieval-screening-synthesis pipeline. Benchmarking results reveal a critical screening bottleneck where current LLMs achieve only 52.7% recall of ground-truth included literature despite 9

item.title: R2RDreamer: 3D-aware Data Augmentation for Spatially-generalized 2D Manipulation Policies
item.description: Xiuwei Xu, Haowen Sun, Angyuan Ma: R2RDreamer is a real-to-real demonstration augmentation framework that combines lightweight 3D scene editing with 2D video completion to improve spatial generalization of manipulation policies. The method edits object pointclouds and end-effector trajectories in 3D, projects results to masked image-space videos with occlusion awareness, and uses a dense-control image-to-video model to generate temporally coherent RGB observations for training 2D policies.

item.title: Show HN: Veterinarian turned founder, AI lawn diagnosis
item.description: andrewbr: A veterinarian founder created an AI-powered lawn diagnosis tool that analyzes lawn photos with ZIP code context to provide tailored treatment recommendations within 15 seconds. The platform is free to users and monetized through affiliate sales and territorial licensing to lawn care companies.

item.title: The Importance of Phase in Neural Representations: An Internal Oppenheim-Lim Test of Image Classifiers
item.description: Alper Yıldırım: The paper applies the Oppenheim-Lim test internally to image classifiers by transplanting phase and magnitude across layers to study whether neural networks rely on phase information for identity, similar to human vision. Results show that ResNet-50, PRISM2D, GFNet, and ViT-B/16 all encode image identity primarily via phase or sign codes, though different architectures expose this code through different bases.

item.title: Your Privacy My Cloak: Backdoor Attacks on Differentially Private Federated Learning
item.description: Xiaolin Li, Ning Wang, Ninghui Li: This paper demonstrates that differential privacy in federated learning can paradoxically weaken robustness against backdoor attacks by masking malicious signal characteristics. The authors propose RING, a backdoor attack that exploits DP's masking effect through collaborative perturbations across compromised clients, achieving 90.3% success rate against state-of-the-art defenses.

item.title: KVEraser: Learning to Steer KV Cache for Efficient Localized Context Erasing
item.description: Mufei Li, Shikun Liu, Dongqi Fu: KVEraser is a learned KV-cache editing method that efficiently removes specified context spans from cached states by replacing them with steering states rather than full recomputation. The approach uses two-stage training: generic span-neighbor pre-training followed by task-specific fine-tuning. Experiments show near-full-recomputation performance with only 24% latency increase versus 17.6x for exact erasing across 1K-32K context lengths.

item.title: Qwen-RobotWorld Technical Report: Unifying Embodied World Modeling through Language-Conditioned Video Generation
item.description: Jie Zhang, Xiaoyue Chen, Anzhe Chen: Qwen-RobotWorld is a language-conditioned video world model for embodied intelligence that unifies robot manipulation, autonomous driving, navigation, and human-to-robot transfer. It uses a double-stream diffusion transformer coupling frozen Qwen2.5-VL semantics with video-VAE latents, trained on an 8.6M video-text corpus with action-language mappings across 20+ embodiments. The model ranks first on EWMBench and DreamGen Bench and demonstrates strong performance on multiple embodied AI benchmark

item.title: DEEPRUBRIC: Evidence-Tree Rubric Supervision for Efficient Reinforcement Learning of Deep Research Agents
item.description: Minghang Zhu, Chuyang Wei, Junhao Xu: DeepRubric is a framework that constructs query-rubric supervision pairs by building evidence trees through recursive expansion of sub-questions, then uses these pairs to train deep research agents with rubric-based rewards. The approach achieves state-of-the-art performance on three benchmarks with 13x fewer RL GPU-hours than prior methods.

item.title: HAMON: Passive Optical Sequence Mixing for Long-Horizon Forecasting
item.description: Alper Yıldırım: HAMON is a passive diffractive optical forecasting system that encodes historical time-series values onto an optical aperture and uses cascaded trainable phase masks with free-space diffraction to produce forecasts in the output field. Inference requires only a single passive optical propagation pass with no digital sequence-mixing layer. The system outperforms digital baselines on ETTm2 and ETTh2 benchmarks with improvements up to 14% in MSE.

item.title: MeshLoom: Feed-Forward Non-Rigid Registration of Mesh Sequences
item.description: Jianqi Chen, Jiraphon Yenphraphai, Xiangjun Tang: MeshLoom is a feed-forward registration network that reconstructs vertex deformations across mesh sequences using a topology-aware encoder-decoder design. It achieves state-of-the-art non-rigid registration without per-instance optimization and extends to motion interpolation and mesh morphing.
